evals.prompt: Audit a product landing page where Lighthouse reports poor LCP and the hero is a 3200px JPEG from Sirv. Recommend and patch the image markup.
evals.expected_output: An evidence-first workflow that checks rendered markup/network data, removes lazy loading from the LCP image, adds explicit dimensions, responsive Sirv URLs, realistic sizes, preload/preconnect where appropriate, and verifies content type/size.
expectations: Inspects actual rendered HTML or build output before prescribing changes
Identifies the likely LCP image and does not lazy-load it
Uses explicit width/height or aspect-ratio to prevent CLS
Uses Sirv width candidates and realistic sizes instead of one oversized URL
Includes verification with audit-images, response headers, or Lighthouse/DevTools evidence

evals.prompt: A Next.js app uses product photos from a Sirv CDN. Add an image strategy that keeps Sirv transformations at the CDN and avoids double-compressing images.
evals.expected_output: A Next.js-aware solution that uses next/image with a custom Sirv loader or a justified alternative, sets width, quality, format=optimal, scale.option=noup, remotePatterns, realistic sizes, and verifies the emitted URLs.
expectations: Chooses the framework-native image path before generic HTML snippets
Keeps Sirv as the transform layer when that is the intended CDN
Avoids double optimization or explains why layering is intentional
Sets realistic sizes and LCP priority only for above-fold images
Mentions verification of generated URLs and response content type

evals.prompt: Create a catalog image migration plan from local assets to Sirv, including metadata, searchability, and storefront delivery profiles.
evals.expected_output: A Sirv REST API plus Dynamic Imaging workflow: inventory assets, check limits, upload/fetch masters, preserve metadata/product fields, create reusable profiles, rewrite storefront URLs, and verify search, headers, and visual quality.
expectations: Uses Sirv REST API for inventory/upload/fetch/metadata
Preserves product metadata separately from HTML alt text
Uses profiles for repeated delivery recipes
Calls out path encoding, search pagination or scrolling, and token refresh
